description: Cat56 density calculation TypeError bug: _h56_density and _a56_density computed via `(gd - r[0]).days` where both gd and r[0] are ISO date strings. Subtracting two strings always raises TypeError, causing the entire Cat56 try/except block to fall through to fallback values. Result: all 10 Cat56 directional circadian features (circ56_*) were always set to constant fallback values [0.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0] — the actual tz-shift and sigmoid-encoded directional signals (lines 6480-6497) were computed but silently discarded. Fix: pre-compute _7ago56 = 7-days-ago ISO string, then use lexicographic comparison (r[0] >= _7ago56) which is valid for YYYY-MM-DD strings. This unblocks all 10 Cat56 features including the westbound/eastbound +8.5pp effect from Chronobiology Int'l 2022.
